errors: ; `StatinMedicationStatements` and `StatinMedicationDispensed` contain duplicate UNION statements for the same Value Sets (e.g., "Low intensity statin therapy" is listed twice).; `StatinMedicationRequests` contains syntax errors (`UNION FROM Retrieve MedicationRequest AS MR`), which is not valid ELM/CQL structure.

[Time Interval Mismatch]: The lookback period for the Colonoscopy procedure is set to 1 month. Clinically, colon cancer screening (colonoscopy) is typically performed every 10 years (or 5 years for high-risk patients). A 1-month lookback is clinically unreasonable as it would trigger a recommendation for a screening almost immediately after a patient has already had one.

The "Assessment" logic is clinically incorrect. It defines the population as patients who have NOT had a screening procedure in the last 1 month. In clinical practice, prostate cancer screening is not performed monthly; a 1-month lookback period is far too short and would trigger a recommendation for almost every patient over 50, regardless of when their last actual screening occurred. Typically, screening intervals are measured in years.

[Time Intervals (Lookback Periods) is incorrectly mapped to a laboratory value (9 g%) instead of a time duration (e.g., days or months). Additionally, the HbA1c threshold of >= 9 g% is unusually high for a general diagnosis threshold, as clinical guidelines typically use >= 6.5% for Type 2 Diabetes diagnosis.]

The "Has Smoking History" logic only checks for an "active" tobacco user status, whereas the "Rationale" and standard clinical guidelines (USPSTF) require a specific quantitative threshold (20 pack-years) regardless of current smoking status.
[Age Thresholds: The minimum age of 60 is clinically outdated for average-risk colorectal cancer screening. Current clinical guidelines (e.g., USPSTF, ACS) recommend initiating screening at age 45.]

[Age Threshold Mismatch]: The 'In Demographic' logic defines the age range as 30-65 years, but the 'Rationale' explicitly states the USPSTF recommendation is for women aged 21-65 years. This creates a clinical gap where patients aged 21-29 are excluded from the population.
[Age Threshold Mismatch: The 'In Demographic' logic is set to Age >= 75, but the 'Rationale' explicitly references the USPSTF recommendation for adults 65 years or older.]

[Age Threshold Mismatch]: The 'In Demographic' logic requires Age >= 25, but the 'Rationale' explicitly states that the USPSTF recommends screening for adults 18 years or older. This creates a clinical gap where patients aged 18-24 are excluded from the population despite the guideline recommendation.

[Age Threshold Mismatch]: The logic definition for 'In Demographic' uses age 55-75, but the 'Rationale' explicitly states the USPSTF recommendation is for men aged 65-75. The ELM implementation is incorrectly triggering for patients aged 55-64.

[Logic Definition Mismatch] The 'MeetsInclusionCriteria' logic uses an OR operator between [Screening Age] and [Gender] (([Screening Age] OR [Gender]) AND [Mammogram]). Clinically, breast cancer screening criteria require the patient to be BOTH the correct age AND the correct gender. As currently written, any female under 40 or any person over 40 regardless of gender would trigger the inclusion criteria.
[The ELM logic for 'In Demographic' only checks for Age >= 50, whereas the 'Rationale' specifies a range of 50-80 years. The ELM is missing the upper age limit of 80.]

The logic for [Needs AAA Screening] and [InPopulation] fails to include the [Is Male] requirement. The Rationale explicitly states the recommendation is for "men aged 65-75 years," but the current logic only checks for age and smoking history, meaning it would incorrectly trigger for female patients.

[The lookback period for Colonoscopy is set to 1 month. Clinically, colon cancer screening (colonoscopy) is typically performed every 10 years (or 5 years for high risk). A 1-month lookback is far too short to determine if a patient is due for screening; it would only identify patients who had a procedure within the last 30 days, which is the opposite of the intended screening population.]

The logic for [Needs Falls Prevention Screening] is defined as [In Demographic] AND [Has Recent Falls Assessment]. This means the system will trigger a recommendation to perform a falls risk assessment only if the patient HAS already had one in the last year. Clinically, the screening should be triggered if the patient is in the age demographic AND has NOT had a recent assessment.

[Logic Error in 'Needs Depression Screening']: The logic currently uses AND between Bipolar and Depression diagnoses ([Has Bipolar Diagnosis] AND [Has Depression Diagnosis]), meaning a patient must have BOTH to be excluded from screening. Clinically, a diagnosis of either Bipolar OR Depression should typically exclude a patient from a general screening trigger.

The logic for `MeetsExclusionCriteria` is defined using an `AND` operator ([Connective Tissue Disorders (SNOMED)] AND [Amiodarones]). Clinically, exclusion criteria for suspected Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF) should be additive (OR). A patient should be excluded if they have a connective tissue disorder OR if they have a history of amiodarone exposure, as either one represents an identifiable secondary cause of interstitial lung disease. As currently written, a patient would only be excluded if they possessed both conditions simultaneously.
